root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Curam Accessibility User Guide
root.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Accessibility User Guide. It includes a note to read the Notices section first, edition/copyright information, and the table of contents. The main content starts with an accessibility overview stating support for WCAG 2.1 AA, Section 508, and EN 301 549, plus supported technologies and keyboard/screen reader guidance. It documents desktop accessibility for multiple-select and single-select lists, the section shortcut panel, tabs, Rich Text Editor, and the classic IEG date picker. It also provides accessible alternatives or workarounds for the Calendar, Express Rule Editor, Data Store Editor, Intelligent Evidence Gathering editor/scripts, browser zoom at 200%, planning workspace, and non-accessible pods. The section ends with legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory note for the document. It tells readers to review the Notices section on page 21 before using the information or the supported product. It also includes the copyright notice for Merative US L.P. (2012, 2024).

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: This section is the front matter and accessibility chapter for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2. It introduces WCAG/Section 508/EN 301 549 compliance, supported technologies, and keyboard/screen reader procedures for desktop features: multiple-select list boxes, single-select lists, section shortcut panel, tabs, Rich Text Editor, and the classic Universal Access date picker. It also gives accessible alternatives for the Calendar and explains accessible Cúram Express Rule Set creation/publishing workflows.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-20
children.summary: Covers accessibility-oriented procedures in Cúram 8.1.2 for Intelligent Evidence Gathering and related areas: creating, modifying, validating, uploading, and overwriting data store schemas and IEG scripts via the Administration Workspace; managing rule sets and associated property files; browser-zoom usability guidance; planning workspace accessibility limitations and alternatives; and accessible replacements for common pod shortcuts (My Cases, Search Person, My Appointments, Create Task). Also notes use of XML editors, import/download actions, validation steps, and panel toggles for low-vision navigation.

Accessibility front matter for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2. Covers WCAG 2.1/Section 508/EN 301 549 compliance, supported technologies, and keyboard/screen reader guidance for desktop controls: multi-select and single-select lists, section shortcut panel, tabs, Rich Text Editor, and the classic Universal Access date picker. Also provides accessible alternatives for Calendar, Express Rule Editor, Data Store Editor, IEG editor/workflows, browser zoom, planning workspace, and pods shortcuts (My Cases, Search Person, My Appointments, Create Task).

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: Merative’s privacy policy is referenced here, with the policy available at https://www.merative.com/privacy. This page is mainly legal/footer material: it includes sample-code copyright and warranty disclaimers, trademark notices for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX, plus a note that sample programs are provided “AS IS” and may be copied or modified under the Merative Client Agreement.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: Lists trademark, copyright, and licensing notices for the document. States that sample names are fictitious, sample programs may be copied and modified under the Merative Client Agreement, and are provided “AS IS” without warranty. Includes the Merative privacy policy URL. Identifies trademarks for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft/Windows, and UNIX, plus a general notice that other names may be trademarks.

Legal notice and permissions for using the publication: personal noncommercial use and internal enterprise use are allowed only if proprietary notices are preserved; derivative works, external distribution, and use outside the enterprise require Merative consent. It disclaims warranties, export/re-export violations, and patent rights, notes no endorsement of external sites, and says non-Merative product information is unverified. Includes the main AS-IS disclaimer and © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for this document. Lists front matter items (Note, Edition) and section 1, “Accessibility in Cúram,” with subsections on accessibility, desktop accessibility features, and accessible desktop alternatives. Also includes page references for topics such as lists, tabs, rich text editor, date picker, calendar, rule editor, data store editor, evidence gathering editor, browser zoom, planning workspace, pods, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: 1 Accessibility in Cúram
1.1 Accessibility
children.summary: Cúram claims accessibility support aligned with WCAG 2.1 AA, Section 508, and EN 301 549, with some known exceptions. It explains that accessibility features are intended to help users with disabilities, especially by supporting screen readers, speech synthesizers, and keyboard-only operation. It also notes that supported prerequisite software should be used for accessibility information, and points to Merative’s broader accessibility commitment via IBM’s Human Ability and Accessibility Center.

children.title: 1.2 Accessibility features for desktop
Multiple-selection list boxes
children.summary: Explains keyboard accessibility for Cúram desktop multiple-selection list boxes in the transfer list and multiple-selection widgets. It describes how Microsoft Edge users can select multiple items with Tab, Arrow keys, Shift+Arrow for adjacent selections, and Ctrl+Alt+Arrow plus Space for nonadjacent selections. The section also briefly introduces single select lists and notes they can be navigated and selected by keyboard or screen reader, including JAWS entering Forms Mode on Enter.

children.title: Single select lists
children.summary: Explains keyboard and screen reader navigation for single select lists in Cúram desktop. Single select lists appear as radio button lists. With a screen reader, tab to a radio button, press Enter to select it (JAWS enters Forms Mode), then use Up/Down Arrow to move and change selection. Also contrasts with non-screen-reader use, continuing the accessibility guidance for keyboard-based selection.

children.title: Section shortcut panel
children.summary: Explains keyboard and screen reader accessibility for the Cúram section shortcut panel. It describes how to expand the panel with Tab and Enter, move to shortcut categories with Tab, browse categories with Up/Down arrows, and access links within a category with Tab. The excerpt also begins a related “Tabs” accessibility topic, covering hierarchical tab navigation and using Tab plus Up/Down arrows to move between tabs. Useful for users needing keyboard navigation guidance in Cúram.

children.title: Tabs
children.summary: Explains keyboard and screen reader navigation for tabs in Cúram. The application uses hierarchical tab navigation at multiple levels: application sections, application tabs, content area navigation bar tabs, and in-page navigation tabs. Only the selected tab is focusable with Tab; use Up/Down arrows to move among tabs in a tab bar. Useful for finding guidance on accessible tab interaction in the UI.

children.title: Rich Text Editor
children.summary: Instructions for keyboard/screen reader use of the Rich Text Editor and related UI controls. Covers how to enter formatted text by tabbing to the editor, using Alt+F10 to focus the toolbar, navigating toolbar options with Tab/arrow keys, selecting with Enter, and exiting with Esc. Also mentions tab behavior (focus, close with Delete/Ctrl+W, context menu with Shift+F10/right-click, expand action menu with Shift+Enter) and begins guidance for using the IEG date picker in the classic Universal Access app with keyboard navigation.

children.title: IEG date picker in the classic Universal Access application
children.summary: Instructions for using the IEG date picker in the classic Universal Access app with keyboard/screen reader support. It explains that users tab to the date picker field, press Down Arrow to open the calendar, and use Left/Right/Up/Down Arrow keys to move between date cells. Useful for finding accessibility and keyboard navigation steps for selecting dates in Cúram Universal Access.

Cúram desktop accessibility guidance for keyboard and screen reader users. Covers multiple-selection list boxes in transfer/multiple-select widgets (Tab, arrows, Shift+Arrow, Ctrl+Alt+Arrow, Space; Edge supported), single-select radio lists (Tab, Enter or Space, Up/Down; JAWS forms mode), section shortcut panel navigation, hierarchical tab navigation and tab closing/action menu shortcuts, Rich Text Editor toolbar access (Alt+F10, arrows, Enter, Esc), and the classic Universal Access IEG date picker (Tab, Down Arrow, arrow-key calendar navigation).

children.title: 1.3 Accessible alternatives for desktop
The Calendar
children.summary: Explains the Calendar’s accessibility limitations and the accessible alternative. The monthly, weekly, and daily calendar views are inaccessible to screen readers, so users should switch to the Calendar List View via the Calendar application section and List View tab to see events in an accessible table. The page also includes keyboard shortcuts for navigating the calendar (Page Up/Down, Alt+Page Up/Down, Home, End, Enter/Space).

children.title: The Cúram Express Rule Editor
Creating a New Rule Set
children.summary: Explains how to create a new CER rule set in the Cúram administration application and then edit, validate, and upload it. It covers navigating to Rules and Evidence, creating the rule set with name/display name/category, extracting it via `creole.extractrulesdata`, editing the XML in a custom component using an XML editor, and finishing with `creole.validate.rulesets` and `creole.upload.rulesets`. Also notes prerequisites like knowledge of CER rule set structure and access to the application database.

children.title: Changing Existing Published Rule Sets
children.summary: Explains how to publish an existing rule set in the Cúram administration application. Users navigate to Administration Workspace Application Section, then Rules and Evidence, and open Cúram Rule Sets to find the target rule set. To complete publishing, the developer must use the command prompt from the <application install directory>/EJBServer folder. The section implies this is an admin/developer activity tied to existing published rule sets, following the workflow for rule set management.

Describes the accessible Cúram Express Rule Editor for creating and publishing CER rule sets in the administration application. It explains that rule sets are XML-based, stored in the database, and typically edited in a custom XML editor outside the online app. Covers prerequisites (CER structure knowledge, database access, use in development/staging) plus workflows for creating new rule sets and publishing existing ones via Administration Workspace > Rules and Evidence, with command-line steps for extract, validate, and upload.

children.title: The Data Store Editor
Creating a Data Store Schema
children.summary: Explains how a user with disabilities can create and import a data store schema in the administration application. The section covers preparing the schema offline in an XML editor, navigating to Administration Workspace > Intelligent Evidence Gathering > Datastore Schema, using the Import page action, and entering the schema name and ID in a modal dialog before browsing to upload the schema file. It also notes that users should understand data store schema structure from the IEG Guide first.

children.title: Modifying Existing Data Store Schemas
children.summary: How to modify an existing data store schema in the Cúram administration application. The procedure covers navigating to Administration Workspace > Intelligent Evidence Gathering > Datastore schema, downloading the current schema, editing it offline in an XML editor, then using Import with Overwrite, entering the original schema name and ID, and uploading the modified file. The system validates the XML before replacing the existing online schema.

Explains accessible creation and modification of data store schemas in the Cúram administration application. Users must first understand schema structure from the IEG Guide and prepare/edit schemas offline in an XML editor. The section covers navigating to Administration Workspace > Intelligent Evidence Gathering > Datastore Schema, using Import to upload a new schema, entering schema name and ID, and browsing to select the file. Child content also covers downloading an existing schema, editing it offline, and re-importing with Overwrite after validation.

children.title: The Intelligent Evidence Gathering Editor
Creation of a New IEG Script
children.summary: Describes how users can create and upload a new IEG script in the administration application. It explains that IEG scripts are created offline using an XML editor, then uploaded through the Administration Workspace via the Intelligent Evidence Gathering category. The script is selected with a browse dialog, validated on upload, and saved only if no XML errors occur. This section is useful for learning the accessible workflow for creating and validating new Intelligent Evidence Gathering scripts.

children.title: Modifying Existing IEG Scripts
children.summary: Explains how to modify an existing IEG script in Cúram’s administration application. Users navigate to Administration Workspace > Intelligent Evidence Gathering > Scripts, search for the script, download it as an offline copy, edit it in an XML editor, and re-import it via the Import page action. The surrounding context also covers validating scripts against a datastore schema and uploading related property/resource files.

This section explains the accessible workflow for creating and modifying Intelligent Evidence Gathering (IEG) scripts in Cúram’s administration application. It covers composing scripts offline in an XML editor, importing them through Administration Workspace > Intelligent Evidence Gathering > Scripts, validating uploaded XML, and searching/downloading existing scripts for offline editing and re-import. It also notes that scripts can be validated against a datastore schema and that related property/resource files can be uploaded via Application Resources.

children.title: Browser zoom
children.summary: Explains browser zoom accessibility in Cúram 8.1.2. At 200% zoom, the Context, Shortcuts, and Smart panels can crowd out navigation tabs and page content; users can collapse these panels with toggle buttons to improve access. Also notes that the planning workspace manages activities and objectives, but drag-and-drop, timeline view, and progress graphs are not fully accessible, so the Objective and Activity tabs should be used instead.

children.title: Planning workspace
children.summary: The Planning workspace is used to manage Activities and Objectives. It notes that some features, including drag and drop, timeline view, and progress graphs, are not fully accessible. For accessible access, users should use the Objective tab and Activity tab to view the same information and perform the same actions.

children.title: Pods
children.summary: Explains that Pods in Cúram are customizable UI widgets used as shortcut containers for viewing information and performing actions, but pods themselves are not accessible. The section provides accessible alternatives to common pod links, such as My Cases, Search for a person, My Appointments, and Create Task, by navigating through tabs, shortcuts panels, list views, and tab actions. Pages 21–22 are legal notices, copyrights, privacy policy, and trademarks.

Covers accessible desktop alternatives in Cúram 8.1.2. Describes screen reader/keyboard-friendly access to the Calendar List View, and accessible workflows for Cúram Express Rule Sets, Data Store Schemas, and IEG scripts via Administration Workspace, XML editors, import/download/overwrite, validation, and upload/publish steps. Notes browser zoom at 200% and collapsing Context/Shortcuts/Smart panels for access. Also explains planning workspace limitations and accessible Objective/Activity tabs. Includes accessible alternatives for nonaccessible Pods and their common links, plus legal notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

Cúram’s accessibility overview states support for WCAG 2.1 AA, Section 508, and EN 301 549, with some exceptions and references to accessibility conformance reports. It explains accessibility aids for screen readers and keyboard-only use, plus supported technologies. The section details desktop keyboard/screen reader navigation for multiple-select lists, single-select radios, section shortcuts, tabs, rich text editing, and the classic IEG date picker. It also covers accessible alternatives for calendar views, CER rules, IEG scripts, browser zoom, planning workspace, and inaccessible pods, followed by notices, privacy, and trademarks.
